text:
## Headline

Nimbus management discussed the pending Cirrus Analytics acquisition, raised full-year operating margin guidance, and reiterated revenue guidance on its earnings call.

## Q&A signals

- On Cirrus integration timing, Daniel Cho said native Cirrus query capabilities are expected to be "generally available inside the Stratus console by the end of fiscal Q4 2026" [Q1].
- On margin drivers, Helena Vargas attributed "about 60 basis points to the hiring slowdown" and "about 40 basis points to underlying gross-margin improvement in Stratus" [Q2].
- On competitive positioning, Daniel Cho noted Nimbus's "win rate against the hyperscaler-native offerings remained above 75 percent in Q2" [Q7].

## Commitments

- Management expects to close the Cirrus Analytics acquisition by the end of fiscal Q3 2026, citing the plan to "close the previously announced Cirrus Analytics acquisition by the end of fiscal Q3 2026" [K1].
- Full-year fiscal 2026 non-GAAP operating margin is now guided to approximately 19%, with management stating they "now expect full-year non-GAAP operating margin to land at approximately 19 percent, up from our prior guidance of 18 percent" [K4].
- Full-year fiscal 2026 revenue guidance was reiterated, with management "reiterating our previously communicated full-year fiscal 2026 revenue guidance of 2.55 to 2.60 billion dollars" [K6].
